author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Implement DeepLabV3 + FCN segmentation models (Sprint C.2, #457). 

Architecture:
1. ASPP module (aspp.rs) — Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling: one 1x1 conv + four 3x3 dilated convs (rates 6,12,18) + global avg pool branch, all projecting to 256 channels, concatenated and projected to 256 channels. Mirrors torchvision ASPP.
2. DeepLabV3 (deeplabv3.rs) — ResNet-50 backbone with dilated convolutions on layer3 (dilation=2) and layer4 (dilation=4, stride=1), ASPP head on layer4 features (2048-ch), 256-ch ASPP output -> 256-ch conv -> num_classes conv.
3. FCN (fcn.rs) — ResNet-50 backbone (standard strides), FCN head on layer4 features (2048-ch): 1x1 conv -> num_classes.
4. segmentation/mod.rs — pub mod declarations + re-exports.
5. models/mod.rs + lib.rs — add pub mod segmentation and re-exports.
6. models/registry.rs — register deeplabv3_resnet50 + fcn_resnet50.
7. ferrotorch-hub/src/registry.rs — add hub entries (placeholder SHA like fasterrcnn).
8. conformance_vision_segmentation.rs — architecture inventory + forward parity + shape checks + registry tests.
9. scripts/regenerate_vision_fixtures.py — extend with segmentation fixtures.

ResNet-50 backbone: reuse existing resnet50 but need a dilated variant for DeepLabV3. Will create ResNet50Dilated wrapper that builds the backbone and overrides layer3/layer4 strides and dilations to match torchvision deeplabv3_resnet50(replace_stride_with_dilation=[False,True,True]).

Output shape: [B, num_classes, H/8, W] for DeepLabV3 after bilinear upsample to input size. Actually torchvision returns full [B, num_classes, H, W] after upsample — but we'll implement without spatial upsample in the core model and add it in forward to match output shape. Wait: torchvision deeplabv3 returns dict {'out': Tensor[B,C,H,W]} but we'll return the tensor directly since ferrotorch has no OrderedDict analog. Shape [B, num_classes, H, W] via bilinear interpolate.

ferrotorch_nn has interpolate available (confirmed in lib.rs upsample module).
